html:
Exclusive to:
This content is available only after activating the Lunar Rift

Contents

  • 1 Undisclosed Construct
  • 2 W.A.R.B.O.T.
  • 3 Summoning (Post-Scion)
  • 4 Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T.
    • 4.1 Fight
      • 4.1.1 Terramites and Warblers
  • 5 Tips
  • 6 Trivia
  • 7 Gallery
  • 8 Sounds

Undisclosed Construct

The Undisclosed Construct spawns in the center of the Hermit Island after filling it completely with Substrate Extrapolators. After giving the Encapsulated Celestial Revenant to the Grainy Transmission, it turns into W.A.R.B.O.T.

W.A.R.B.O.T.

W.A.R.B.O.T. is a structure that is unveiled after giving an Encapsulated Celestial Revenant to the Grainy Transmission. When it is unveiled, the Fulcronial Selector appears next to it, and the Grainy Transmission prompts the player to interact with it. Upon interacting with the Fulcronial Selector, W.A.R.B.O.T. activates and wanders around. A few seconds after it activates, it is corrupted by lunar power and becomes Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T., a boss monster.

Summoning (Post-Scion)

This content is available only after defeating Celestial Scion

After defeating Celestial Scion for the first time, the W.A.R.B.O.T. Base Kit and W.A.R.B.O.T. Parts can then be crafted at the Notional Fabricator. W.A.R.B.O.T. can be assembled by placing the W.A.R.B.O.T. Base Kit at the center of the island, then providing it with three W.A.R.B.O.T. Parts.

Six Phasmo-Encapsulators (with a Gestalt, Grazer or Incursive Gestalt trapped within) must be placed around the W.A.R.B.O.T. Upon refighting the boss, any type of captured Gestalt can be placed, including six of any specific kind of Gestalt (e.g. six Warblers or six Terramites), unlike the initial fight where Wagstaff would limit you to five of a kind.

Placing the last Gestalt will summon the Celestial Revenant, which must be defeated, captured and placed within the W.A.R.B.O.T. to reveal the Fulcronial Selector, which can then be activated to start the W.A.R.B.O.T. fight.

Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T.

“These heat-seeking missiles lock onto the warmest thing they can find... So make sure that isn't you!”

-Thermalock Missile

Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. is a boss monster that spawns from activating the Fulcronial Selector.

Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. has Planar Entity Protection.

After being defeated, the Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. discards its shell and leaves three W.A.R.B.O.T. Legs on the ground, revealing the Celestial Scion inside. It also has drops a Gears (50%), a Electrical Doodad (50%), a Scrap with 50% chance to drop a second one, and a Sketch for W.A.R.B.O.T. Figure.

Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. will determine its attack period based on the rate at which its health declines:

DPS ≤ 225 225 ≤ DPS < 300 300 ≤ DPS < 375 ≥ 375
Attack Period 3.5 3 2.5 2

Fight

Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. has four attacks, in addition to the continuous attacks of its Terramites and Warblers:

  • Slam: Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. slams itself into the ground, dealing damage to any players right next to it and Knockback them. It only uses this attack if it has a target close to it.
  • Jump: Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. jumps in place three times to telegraph its attack, then flies through the air to land on the player and knockback them. This attack can be easily dodged by standing still and moving right before it hits, or by teleporting with a Lazy Explorer or Wortox's Souls/Wanda's Backtrek watch.
  • Heat-Seeking Missiles: After reaching 17,000 or less, Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. sends out several heat-seeking missiles. Once Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. has started this attack, it will not stop until a specific amount of damage[Clarification Needed] is dealt, after which it will continue its normal pattern.
    • If target lock fails during W.A.R.B.O.T.’s missile phase, it will skip ahead to its next attack phase. (Missiles that failed to lock onto a target will just detonate at random points on the ground.)
    • The missiles target any heat source, which includes fires, Dwarf Star, hot Thermal Stones, Winona's Generators and G.E.Merator, Scaled Furnace and players that aren't actively Freezing. It is nearly impossible to dodge a missile that is targeting the player.
    • Once hit, the missiles will put out fire, extinguish Dwarf Star, and cost the durability of Thermal Stones. If hit on structures, it will destroy them. Targets that were previously extinguished by a missile will no longer smoulder (although they can still be ignited manually by the player).
    • If all the players keep freezing during the whole fight, Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. will fail to use this attack.
  • Beam: After reaching 9,000 or less, Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. spawns a circular light wave at the top of its head, signaling to the sky to create a high-energy beam attack centered at the player. This beam deals 40/s of *Lunar Burning Damage to every player and mobs that stands on it. This can be easily dodged by a player who predicts it and continuously moves. The Beam will heal Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. if hits it.
      • Lunar Burning Damage: A continuous damage similar to Fire damage, is affected by Planar Defense and the Damage Taken Modifiers for Lunar Aligned players and mobs. It will also make mobs panic. The damage of lunar burning is determined with this formula:
DPS = 40 × ∏ Damage Taken Modifiers for Lunar Aligned - Σ Planar Defense / 4

Terramites and Warblers

After reaching 14,500, Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. wakes up all Terramites and Warblers around it. Both types circle around Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T., but each has its own attacks.

Terramites will spin around Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. If one touches the player, it deals damage unless the player is wearing a hard armor, in which case it has a 50% chance to bounce and deal zero damage. Sometimes[Clarification Needed], all Terramites will stop spinning temporarily and activate lightning barriers between each other. Every Terramite creates a barrier between itself and the 3-4 nearest other Terramites.

Warblers will fly around Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. and periodically shoot a projectile straight down, dealing 45 electric damage.

Tips

  • When spawning Celestial Revenant, it is important to consider how many Terramites and Warblers to use. The total number must add up to six, but the player can decide how many of each to use.
    • Many players consider Warblers to be easier to fight than Terramites, since they can only deal damage periodically. These players often fight with either 5 or 6 Warblers and 0 or 1 Terramites.
    • However, Terramites are more useful than Warblers, since they can be used to farm resources after the fight is over, so it may be better to use Terramites. Using three Terramites and three Warblers pairs nicely, since one can hammer the three Warblers afterward for three Gyroscopic Transduction Core to repair the three Terramites.
    • If one doesn't care about farming resources with Terramites or the difficulty of the Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. fight, the easiest approach is to use six Terramites, as Gestalts are generally easier to acquire than Greater Gestalts or Grazers.
  • It is recommended to kill Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. near the center of the Hermit Island, as that locates the three W.A.R.B.O.T. Legs in a favorable position for the Celestial Scion fight.
  • Intentionally freezing oneself with an Ice Crystaleyezer is a great way of avoiding the heat-seeking missiles. Jellybeans work very well to counteract the freezing damage.
  • WX-78 with a Refrigerant Circuit can stop the heat-seeking missiles from targeting them.
  • Since Celestial Scion has an Enlightenment meter and Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. doesn't, it is recommended to lower one's Sanity towards the end of the Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. fight so that it isn't maxed out for the start of the Celestial Scion fight.
    • The Shadow Maul does this nicely, especially in conjunction with the Ice Crystaleyezer strategy described above.
  • Because W.A.R.B.O.T. is immune to all electrical damage, electrical items or buff will be affected negatively.
  • Woodie's Weremoose, Wigfrid and Warly's Garlic Powder buff has a resistance to all damage, which can help mitigate the Lunar Burning Damage.

Trivia

  • The Undisclosed Construct, W.A.R.B.O.T., and Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. were added in the Hostile Takeover update.
  • In the early beta, after defeating the Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T., only a Beta Report drops. The report looks the same as a Map Scroll. When examining, it says "Thanks for playing the beta! Boss final phase and rewards coming soon... You defeated Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. in {time}s."
    • Later in the beta, after Celestial Scion was added, Beta Report no longer drops from Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T., but it can still be spawned in with code c_spawn("temp_beta_msg").

Gallery

  • On Lunar Quest Concept Art from Hostile Takeover update stream from Rhymes with Play.
  • W.A.R.B.O.T. Concept Art from Hostile Takeover update stream from Rhymes with Play.
  • W.A.R.B.O.T. Concept Art from Hostile Takeover update stream from Rhymes with Play.
  • W.A.R.B.O.T. Attacks Concept Art from Hostile Takeover update stream from Rhymes with Play.
  • W.A.R.B.O.T. Concept Animation from Hostile Takeover update stream from Rhymes with Play.
  • W.A.R.B.O.T. Concept Animation from Hostile Takeover update stream from Rhymes with Play.
  • W.A.R.B.O.T. Concept Animation from Hostile Takeover update stream from Rhymes with Play.
  • Bombs Concept from Hostile Takeover update stream from Rhymes with Play.
  • Celestial Scion Appear Animation from Hostile Takeover update stream from Rhymes with Play.
  • On Main Menu Concept Art from Hostile Takeover update stream from Rhymes with Play.

Sounds

Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. Battle Theme

Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. is a boss monster that spawns from activating the Fulcronial Selector.
Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. has Planar Entity Protection.
After being defeated, the Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. discards its shell and leaves three W.A.R.B.O.T. Legs on the ground, revealing the Celestial Scion inside. It also has drops a Gears (50%), a Electrical Doodad (50%), a Scrap with 50% chance to drop a second one, and a Sketch for W.A.R.B.O.T. Figure.
Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. will determine its attack period based on the rate at which its health declines:

Fight[edit | edit source]
Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. has four attacks, in addition to the continuous attacks of its Terramites and Warblers:
Slam: Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. slams itself into the ground, dealing damage to any players right next to it and Knockback them. It only uses this attack if it has a target close to it.
Jump: Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. jumps in place three times to telegraph its attack, then flies through the air to land on the player and knockback them. This attack can be easily dodged by standing still and moving right before it hits, or by teleporting with a Lazy Explorer or Wortox's Souls/Wanda's Backtrek watch.
Heat-Seeking Missiles: After reaching 17,000 or less, Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. sends out several heat-seeking missiles. Once Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. has started this attack, it will not stop until a specific amount of damage[Clarification Needed] is dealt, after which it will continue its normal pattern.
If target lock fails during W.A.R.B.O.T.’s missile phase, it will skip ahead to its next attack phase. (Missiles that failed to lock onto a target will just detonate at random points on the ground.)
The missiles target any heat source, which includes fires, Dwarf Star, hot Thermal Stones, Winona's Generators and G.E.Merator, Scaled Furnace and players that aren't actively Freezing. It is nearly impossible to dodge a missile that is targeting the player.
Once hit, the missiles will put out fire, extinguish Dwarf Star, and cost the durability of Thermal Stones. If hit on structures, it will destroy them. Targets that were previously extinguished by a missile will no longer smoulder (although they can still be ignited manually by the player).
If all the players keep freezing during the whole fight, Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. will fail to use this attack.
Beam: After reaching 9,000 or less, Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. spawns a circular light wave at the top of its head, signaling to the sky to create a high-energy beam attack centered at the player. This beam deals 40/s of *Lunar Burning Damage to every player and mobs that stands on it. This can be easily dodged by a player who predicts it and continuously moves. The Beam will heal Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. if hits it.
Lunar Burning Damage: A continuous damage similar to Fire damage, is affected by Planar Defense and the Damage Taken Modifiers for Lunar Aligned players and mobs. It will also make mobs panic. The damage of lunar burning is determined with this formula:
DPS = 40 × ∏ Damage Taken Modifiers for Lunar Aligned - Σ Planar Defense / 4
Terramites and Warblers[edit | edit source]
After reaching 14,500, Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. wakes up all Terramites and Warblers around it. Both types circle around Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T., but each has its own attacks.
Terramites will spin around Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. If one touches the player, it deals damage unless the player is wearing a hard armor, in which case it has a 50% chance to bounce and deal zero damage. Sometimes[Clarification Needed], all Terramites will stop spinning temporarily and activate lightning barriers between each other. Every Terramite creates a barrier between itself and the 3-4 nearest other Terramites.
Warblers will fly around Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. and periodically shoot a projectile straight down, dealing 45 electric damage.
Tips[edit | edit source]
When spawning Celestial Revenant, it is important to consider how many Terramites and Warblers to use. The total number must add up to six, but the player can decide how many of each to use.
Many players consider Warblers to be easier to fight than Terramites, since they can only deal damage periodically. These players often fight with either 5 or 6 Warblers and 0 or 1 Terramites.
However, Terramites are more useful than Warblers, since they can be used to farm resources after the fight is over, so it may be better to use Terramites. Using three Terramites and three Warblers pairs nicely, since one can hammer the three Warblers afterward for three Gyroscopic Transduction Core to repair the three Terramites.
If one doesn't care about farming resources with Terramites or the difficulty of the Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. fight, the easiest approach is to use six Terramites, as Gestalts are generally easier to acquire than Greater Gestalts or Grazers.
It is recommended to kill Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. near the center of the Hermit Island, as that locates the three W.A.R.B.O.T. Legs in a favorable position for the Celestial Scion fight.
Intentionally freezing oneself with an Ice Crystaleyezer is a great way of avoiding the heat-seeking missiles. Jellybeans work very well to counteract the freezing damage.
WX-78 with a Refrigerant Circuit can stop the heat-seeking missiles from targeting them.
Since Celestial Scion has an Enlightenment meter and Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. doesn't, it is recommended to lower one's Sanity towards the end of the Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. fight so that it isn't maxed out for the start of the Celestial Scion fight.
The Shadow Maul does this nicely, especially in conjunction with the Ice Crystaleyezer strategy described above.
Because W.A.R.B.O.T. is immune to all electrical damage, electrical items or buff will be affected negatively.
Woodie's Weremoose, Wigfrid and Warly's Garlic Powder buff has a resistance to all damage, which can help mitigate the Lunar Burning Damage.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
The Undisclosed Construct, W.A.R.B.O.T., and Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. were added in the Hostile Takeover update.
In the early beta, after defeating the Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T., only a Beta Report drops. The report looks the same as a Map Scroll. When examining, it says "Thanks for playing the beta! Boss final phase and rewards coming soon... You defeated Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. in {time}s."
Later in the beta, after Celestial Scion was added, Beta Report no longer drops from Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T., but it can still be spawned in with code c_spawn("temp_beta_msg").

When it is unveiled, the [[Fulcronial Selector]] appears next to it, and the Grainy Transmission prompts the player to interact with it. Base Kit]] and [[W.A.R.B.O.T. Parts]] can then be crafted at the [[Notional Fabricator]]. W.A.R.B.O.T. can be assembled by placing the W.A.R.B.O.T. Base Kit at the center of the island, then providing it with three W.A.R.B.O.T. Parts. Six [[Phasmo-Encapsulator#Encapsulated Gestalts|Phasmo-Encapsulator]]s (with a [[Gestalt]], [[Grazer]] or [[Incursive Gestalt]] trapped within) must be placed around the W.A.R.B.O.T. Upon refighting the boss, any type of captured Gestalt can be placed, including six of any specific kind of Gestalt (e.g. six [[Warbler]]s or six [[Terramite]]s), unlike the initial fight where Wagstaff would limit you to five of a kind. Placing the last Gestalt will summon the [[Celestial Revenant]], which must be defeated, captured and placed within the W.A.R.B.O.T. to reveal the [[Fulcronial Selector]], which can then be activated to start the W.A.R.B.O.T. fight. == Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. == {{Object Infobox |name = Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. |image = Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T..png |health = 22500 |lunarAligned=1 |planarEntityProtection = Yes |attackPeriod=See the main text |spawnFrom = {{pic|32|W.A.R.B.O.T.}} |spawns = {{pic|32|Celestial Scion}} |drops = {{pic32|Gears}}×0-1, {{pic32|Electrical Doodad/DST}}×0-1, {{pic32|Scrap}}×1-2, {{pic|32|W.A.R.B.O.T. Sketch}} for {{pic|32|W.A.R.B.O.T. Figure}} |spawnCode="wagboss_robot" }} {{Quotes |wilson = Has anyone tried resetting it? |willow = I knew it was a killer robot! |wolfgang = No fair! Wolfgang no have rockets! |wendy = I would be angry too. |wx78 = I THOUGHT WE HAD SOMETHING! |wickerbottom = The entity does not enjoy being trapped and enslaved. |woodie = Hey, quit tryin' to kill us! |maxwell = I told you so! |wigfrid = The förged beast is cursed! |webber = We just wanted to play! |winona = Where's the dang OFF switch?! |warly = Sorry! I'm sure you make great soufflés! |wortox = It's got a few screws loose, I do deduce. |wormwood = Bad friend! Bad friend! |wurt = Flort! Flort! Flort! Bad Ironfolk! |walter = It's a bot unstable! Get it, Woby? |wanda = We never learn! |choose = wx78 }} {{Scrapbook Info|These heat-seeking missiles lock onto the warmest thing they can find... So make sure that isn't you!|Thermalock Missile}} '''Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T.''' is a boss monster that spawns from activating the [[Fulcronial Selector]]. Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. has [[Planar Entity Protection]]. After being defeated, the Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. discards its shell and leaves three [[W.A.R.B.O.T. Leg]]s on the ground, revealing the [[Celestial Scion]] inside. It also has drops a [[Gears/DST|Gears]] (50%), a [[Electrical Doodad/DST|Electrical Doodad]] (50%), a [[Scrap]] with 50% chance to drop a second one, and a [[Sketch]] for [[W.A.R.B.O.T. Figure]]. Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. will determine its attack period based on the rate at which its health declines: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" !DPS |≤ 225|| 225 ≤ DPS < 300 || 300 ≤ DPS < 375 || ≥ 375 |- !Attack Period |3.5||3||2.5||2 |} === {{Pic|32|Spear}} Fight === Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. has four attacks, in addition to the continuous attacks of its [[Terramite]]s and [[Warbler]]s: *'''Slam''': Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. slams itself into the ground, dealing damage to any players right next to it and [[Knockback]] them. It only uses this attack if it has a target close to it. *'''Jump''': Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. jumps in place three times to telegraph its attack, then flies through the air to land on the player and knockback them. This attack can be easily dodged by standing still and moving right before it hits, or by teleporting with a [[Lazy Explorer]] or [[Wortox/Don't Starve Together|Wortox's]] [[Soul]]s/[[Wanda/Don't Starve Together|Wanda's]] [[Backtrek Watch|Backtrek]] watch. *'''Heat-Seeking Missiles''': After reaching 17,000{{hp}} or less, Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. sends out several heat-seeking missiles. Once Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. has started this attack, it will not stop until a specific amount of damage{{clarify|how much?}} is dealt, after which it will continue its normal pattern. ** If target lock fails during W.A.R.B.O.T.’s missile phase, it will skip ahead to its next attack phase. (Missiles that failed to lock onto a target will just detonate at random points on the ground.) **The missiles target any heat source, which includes fires, [[Dwarf Star]], hot [[Thermal Stone/DST|Thermal Stones]], [[Winona's Generator|Winona's Generators]] and [[Winona's G.E.M.erator|G.E.Merator]], [[Scaled Furnace]] and players that aren't actively [[Freezing]]. It is nearly impossible to dodge a missile that is targeting the player. ** Once hit, the missiles will put out fire, extinguish Dwarf Star, and cost the durability of Thermal Stones. If hit on structures, it will destroy them. Targets that were previously extinguished by a missile will no longer smoulder (although they can still be ignited manually by the player). ** If all the players keep freezing during the whole fight, Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. will fail to use this attack. *'''Beam''': After reaching 9,000{{hp}} or less, Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. spawns a circular light wave at the top of its head, signaling to the sky to create a high-energy beam attack centered at the player. This beam deals 40{{hp}}/s of *Lunar Burning Damage to every player and mobs that stands on it. This can be easily dodged by a player who predicts it and continuously moves. The Beam will heal Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. if hits it. ***Lunar Burning Damage: A continuous damage similar to [[Fire damage]], is affected by [[Planar Defense]] and the [[Damage/DST|Damage Taken Modifiers]] for [[Lunar Aligned]] players and mobs. It will also make mobs panic. The damage of lunar burning is determined with this formula: ::DPS = 40 × ∏ Damage Taken Modifiers for Lunar Aligned - Σ Planar Defense / 4 ==== Terramites and Warblers ==== After reaching 14,500{{hp}}, Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. wakes up all [[Terramite]]s and [[Warbler]]s around it. Both types circle around Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T., but each has its own attacks. Terramites will spin around Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. If one touches the player, it deals damage unless the player is wearing a [[Armor/DST#Hard_Armor|hard armor]], in which case it has a 50% chance to bounce and deal zero damage. Sometimes{{Clarify}}, all Terramites will stop spinning temporarily and activate lightning barriers between each other. Every Terramite creates a barrier between itself and the 3-4 nearest other Terramites. Warblers will fly around Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. and periodically shoot a projectile straight down, dealing 45 electric damage. {{clear}} == {{Tips}} Tips == * When spawning [[Celestial Revenant]], it is important to consider how many [[Terramite]]s and [[Warbler]]s to use. The total number must add up to six, but the player can decide how many of each to use. ** Many players consider Warblers to be easier to fight than Terramites, since they can only deal damage periodically. These players often fight with either 5 or 6 Warblers and 0 or 1 Terramites. ** However, Terramites are more useful than Warblers, since they can be used to farm resources after the fight is over, so it may be better to use Terramites. Using three Terramites and three Warblers pairs nicely, since one can hammer the three Warblers afterward for three [[Gyroscopic Transduction Core]] to repair the three Terramites. ** If one doesn't care about farming resources with Terramites or the difficulty of the Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. fight, the easiest approach is to use six Terramites, as [[Gestalt]]s are generally easier to acquire than [[Greater Gestalt]]s or [[Grazer]]s. * It is recommended to kill Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. near the center of the [[Hermit_Island#Wagstaff_Questline|Hermit Island]], as that locates the three [[W.A.R.B.O.T. Leg]]s in a favorable position for the [[Celestial Scion]] fight. * Intentionally freezing oneself with an [[Ice Crystaleyezer]] is a great way of avoiding the heat-seeking missiles. [[Jellybeans]] work very well to counteract the freezing damage. * WX-78 with a [[Refrigerant Circuit]] can stop the heat-seeking missiles from targeting them. * Since [[Celestial Scion]] has an [[Enlightenment]] meter and Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. doesn't, it is recommended to lower one's [[Sanity]] towards the end of the Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. fight so that it isn't maxed out for the start of the Celestial Scion fight. ** The [[Shadow Maul]] does this nicely, especially in conjunction with the Ice Crystaleyezer strategy described above. *Because W.A.R.B.O.T. is immune to all electrical damage, electrical items or buff will be affected negatively. *[[Weremoose|Woodie's Weremoose]], [[Wigfrid]] and [[Garlic Powder|Warly's Garlic Powder]] buff has a resistance to all damage, which can help mitigate the Lunar Burning Damage. == {{Trivia}} Trivia == * The Undisclosed Construct, W.A.R.B.O.T., and Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. were added in the ''[[Hostile Takeover]]'' update. * In the early beta, after defeating the Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T., only a Beta Report drops. The report looks the same as a [[Map Scroll]]. When examining, it says "Thanks for playing the beta! Boss final phase and rewards coming soon... You defeated Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T. in {time}s." ** Later in the beta, after [[Celestial Scion]] was added, Beta Report no longer drops from Enlightened W.A.R.B.O.T., but it can still be spawned in with code c_spawn("temp_beta_msg"). == [[File:Blueprint.png|32px]] Gallery == RWP HT Lunar Quest Concept Art.png|On Lunar Quest Concept Art from Hostile Takeover update stream from ''Rhymes with Play''.
